message:
feat: comprehensive unit test suite for auto-repair patterns (bd-k5q5.8.9)

New dedicated test file with 32 tests organized by pattern:
- Pattern 1: dist→src fallback (2 tests)
- Pattern 2: missing asset fallback (3 tests)
- Pattern 3: monorepo escape stubs (7 unit + 2 integration)
- Pattern 5: export shape normalization (4 tests)
- Cross-pattern: repair mode gating (4 tests)
- Pattern classification (5 tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -740,7 +740,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.8.6","title":"Pattern 5: export shape normalization for CJS/ESM translation issues","description":"## Problem

Some extensions fail with "not a function" when the runtime tries to call
the default export.  This happens because of CJS/ESM translation mismatches:

- Extension uses \`module.exports = function(pi) { ... }\` (CJS pattern)
- Our maybe_cjs_to_esm() converts this but sometimes produces
  \`export default { default: function(pi) { ... } }\` (double-wrapped)
- Or the extension uses \`exports.activate = function(pi) { ... }\` instead
  of a default export

### Real examples
- npm/aliou-pi-guardrails: failed because configLoader.getConfig() wasn't
  a function (now fixed by ConfigLoader stub, but the general pattern persists)
- npm/aliou-pi-toolchain: same ConfigLoader issue
- Some community extensions use \`export function activate(pi)\` instead of
  \`export default function(pi)\`

## Solution

In the \`__pi_load_extension\` JS function (extensions_js.rs), add fallback
logic when calling the default export fails:

```javascript
async function __pi_load_extension(entryPath, metadata) {
    const mod = await import(entryPath);

    // Try calling default export
    let activator = mod.default;

    // Pattern 5a: double-wrapped default
    if (activator && typeof activator !== 'function' && typeof activator.default === 'function') {
        activator = activator.default;
        // emit repair event
    }

    // Pattern 5b: named activate/init export
    if (typeof activator !== 'function') {
        if (typeof mod.activate === 'function') {
            activator = mod.activate;
            // emit repair event
        } else if (typeof mod.init === 'function') {
            activator = mod.init;
            // emit repair event
        } else if (typeof mod.setup === 'function') {
            activator = mod.setup;
            // emit repair event
        }
    }

    if (typeof activator !== 'function') {
        throw new Error(
            'Extension default export is not a function. ' +
            'Expected: export default function(pi) { ... } or ' +
            'export function activate(pi) { ... }'
        );
    }

    await activator(__pi_create_api(metadata));
}
```

## Security Analysis

- SAFE: We're only changing WHICH function gets called, not granting any
  new capabilities.  The function still receives the same sandboxed API object.
- The fallback only tries a fixed set of known lifecycle method names
  (default, activate, init, setup).  No arbitrary property access.
- If none of the known patterns match, the extension still fails with a
  clear error message.

## Also: improve maybe_cjs_to_esm()

The root cause of some double-wrapping is in maybe_cjs_to_esm() which
handles \`module.exports = ...\`.  We should audit this function to ensure
it doesn't produce double-wrapped defaults.  Specifically:

- \`module.exports = fn\` → \`export default fn\` (correct)
- \`module.exports = { default: fn }\` → should unwrap to \`export default fn\`
- \`exports.default = fn\` → \`export default fn\` (correct)

## Testing

1. Unit test: double-wrapped default is unwrapped
2. Unit test: named activate export is used as fallback
3. Unit test: named init export is used as fallback
4. Unit test: completely non-callable export still throws clear error
5. Unit test: repair event emitted with ExportShape pattern
6. Unit test: maybe_cjs_to_esm doesn't double-wrap module.exports = { default: fn }
7. Regression: all 223 extensions still pass

## Estimated Effort: 2-3 hours

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions_js.rs (__pi_load_extension JS function, maybe_cjs_to_esm)

## Dependencies
- Requires bd-k5q5.8.1 (logging infrastructure) for repair event emission
- Independent of Patterns 1-4","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:18.988843552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:04:01.727038490Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:04:01.726946809Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auto-repair","cjs-esm","extensions","module-loading"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:18.988843552Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:45.408447637Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":868,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Details: Export Shape Normalization

### The Problem
Some extensions export their lifecycle functions in non-standard ways due to
CJS/ESM translation issues. The most common scenarios:
- Default export is an object wrapping the init function: `{ default: { init } }`
- Default export is the init function directly but module.exports isn't set
- Named exports exist but default export is undefined
- Double-wrapping from bundler output: `{ default: { default: init } }`

### Affected Extensions
- aliou-pi-guardrails: `init` was not callable due to export shape
- aliou-pi-toolchain: similar CJS/ESM mismatch
- Various npm-registry extensions with bundler output

### Current Manual Fix
In the conformance test runner and extension loader, we rely on the module having
a callable default export with an `init` method. Extensions that don't match this
shape simply fail. The manual fix was correcting VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json to not
expect registrations from extensions that fail this way.

### Auto-Repair Implementation

Location: Extension loader in `src/extensions_js.rs`, specifically where we
call the extension's init/activate function.

After loading the module, before calling init:
```javascript
function normalizeExtensionExport(mod) {
    // Already a function with init → use as-is
    if (typeof mod === 'function') return mod;
    if (typeof mod?.init === 'function') return mod;

    // Unwrap { default: X } from CJS interop
    if (mod?.default) {
        if (typeof mod.default === 'function') return mod.default;
        if (typeof mod.default?.init === 'function') return mod.default;
        // Double-wrapped: { default: { default: X } }
        if (mod.default?.default) {
            if (typeof mod.default.default === 'function') return mod.default.default;
            if (typeof mod.default.default?.init === 'function') return mod.default.default;
        }
    }

    // Try named exports as fallback
    if (typeof mod?.activate === 'function') return { init: mod.activate };
    if (typeof mod?.setup === 'function') return { init: mod.setup };

    return mod; // Give up, return original (will fail naturally)
}
```

### Where This Runs
In `run_extension_init()` or equivalent in extensions_js.rs, after the module
is loaded via QuickJS but before we call `.init(api)`:
```rust
// After eval_module():
let raw_export = get_default_export(module);
// Apply normalization:
let normalized = call_normalize_extension_export(raw_export);
// Now call init:
call_init(normalized, api);
```

### Heuristic Priority (highest to lowest)
1. Direct callable with init method (standard) - no repair needed
2. Unwrap one level of { default: X } - very common CJS interop pattern
3. Unwrap two levels of { default: { default: X } } - bundler artifacts
4. Try 'activate' named export (VS Code extension convention)
5. Try 'setup' named export (alternative convention)
6. Give up and let original error propagate

### Security Notes
- Normalization only restructures the export object, no new code execution
- The init function that gets called is still the extension's own code
- We're just finding the right reference to call, not injecting behavior
- Every normalization step is logged as a repair event

### Testing
- Unit: standard export shape → no normalization applied
- Unit: { default: initFn } → unwraps correctly
- Unit: { default: { default: initFn } } → double-unwrap works
- Unit: { activate: fn } → wrapped as { init: fn }
- Unit: no valid shape → returns original (natural failure)
- Unit: repair_config.export_shape = false → no normalization
- Integration: load aliou-pi-guardrails → succeeds via normalization","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:36:52Z"},{"id":926,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Additional Deliverable: Audit maybe_cjs_to_esm()

The bead description mentions fixing maybe_cjs_to_esm() as a secondary task, but
this should be explicit in the acceptance criteria.

### What to Audit

`maybe_cjs_to_esm()` in `extensions_js.rs` converts CommonJS patterns to ESM.
The known problematic case:

Input:  `module.exports = { default: function(pi) { ... } }`
Current output: `export default { default: function(pi) { ... } }` (DOUBLE-WRAPPED!)
Correct output: `export default function(pi) { ... }` (unwrapped)

### Specific Check

Find the code that handles `module.exports = <object literal>` and add a check:
if the object literal has a single key named 'default', unwrap it.

### Updated Acceptance Criteria (additions)

- [ ] maybe_cjs_to_esm() handles module.exports = { default: fn } correctly
- [ ] Test: CJS input with { default: fn } produces single-wrapped ESM
- [ ] Test: CJS input with { a: fn, b: fn } keeps object as-is
- [ ] Test: existing CJS extensions in corpus still load correctly after fix

### Risk

Changing maybe_cjs_to_esm() could break existing extensions that work today.
Run the full 223-extension conformance after any change. If any existing extension
breaks, the fix in maybe_cjs_to_esm() is too aggressive — fall back to only doing
the normalization at load time (the primary Pattern 5 approach).","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:53:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","title":"Integration tests: verify auto-repair with real extensions","description":"## What

After all 5 patterns are implemented, create an integration test suite that
verifies auto-repair works end-to-end with real extension artifacts.  The test
should:

1. Remove the manual stubs we created (shared/index.ts, dist/extension.js,
   form/*.html, constants.ts, utils.ts, processes-component.ts)
2. Load the previously-broken extensions
3. Verify they load successfully via auto-repair
4. Verify the correct repair events are emitted
5. Verify the extensions register the expected commands/tools/hooks

## Test Structure

\`tests/extension_auto_repair.rs\`:

```rust
#[test]
fn auto_repair_dist_to_src_fallback() {
    // Remove dist/extension.js stub for community/qualisero-pi-agent-scip
    // Load the extension
    // Verify it loads and registers expected items
    // Verify RepairEvent(DistToSrc) was emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_missing_asset() {
    // Remove form/ stubs for nicobailon-interview-tool
    // Load the extension
    // Verify it loads (server.ts gets empty HTML/CSS/JS)
    // Verify RepairEvent(MissingAsset) was emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_monorepo_escape() {
    // Remove shared/index.ts stub
    // Load qualisero-background-notify
    // Verify it loads with auto-generated stub
    // Verify RepairEvent(MonorepoEscape) was emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_proxy_stub() {
    // Remove a hand-written virtual module
    // Load extension that depends on it
    // Verify it loads via proxy stub
    // Verify RepairEvent(MissingNpmDep) was emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_disabled() {
    // Set auto_repair_enabled = false
    // Try loading a broken extension
    // Verify it fails with the original error
    // Verify no repair events emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_never_stubs_system_modules() {
    // Verify that node:fs, child_process, etc. are never proxy-stubbed
    // Even when auto_repair is enabled
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] At least 6 integration tests covering all 5 patterns + disabled mode
- [ ] Tests use real extension artifacts (not synthetic)
- [ ] Tests verify both success (extension loads) and observability (events emitted)
- [ ] Tests verify the kill-switch works (auto_repair_enabled = false)
- [ ] All tests pass in CI

## Why This Matters

The integration tests are our proof that auto-repair works correctly and safely.
They're also the regression safety net — if a future change breaks auto-repair,
these tests catch it.

## Estimated Effort: 3-4 hours

## Files to Create
- tests/extension_auto_repair.rs

## Dependencies
- Requires ALL 5 patterns to be implemented
- Requires bd-k5q5.8.1 (logging), bd-k5q5.8.2 (P1), bd-k5q5.8.3 (P2),
  bd-k5q5.8.4 (P3), bd-k5q5.8.5 (P4), bd-k5q5.8.6 (P5)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:14.532673214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:47:00.787612287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auto-repair","extensions","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:14.532673214Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:18.302062857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:21.559483168Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:24.855352435Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:27.478677090Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:29.791234320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:47:00.787548027Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":869,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Details: Integration Tests

### Test Strategy
Integration tests verify the auto-repair system works end-to-end with REAL
extension source code from the conformance corpus. They are the gatekeeper
before we graduate manual stubs (bd-k5q5.8.8).

### Test Structure
File: `tests/ext_auto_repair.rs` (new file, feature-gated with `ext-conformance`)

### Test Scenarios

**1. dist→src fallback (Pattern 1)**
- Remove the manual `dist/extension.js` stub from qualisero-pi-agent-scip
- Load the extension with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: extension loads successfully
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=DistToSrc is emitted
- Assert: the resolved path points to `src/extension.ts`

**2. Missing asset fallback (Pattern 2)**
- Remove the manual `form/` directory from nicobailon-interview-tool
- Load the extension with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: extension loads (readFileSync returns empty string)
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=MissingAsset is emitted for each file

**3. Monorepo escape (Pattern 3)**
- Remove the manual `shared/index.ts` stub
- Load qualisero-background-notify with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: extension loads successfully
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=MonorepoEscape is emitted
- Assert: the generated stub exports match what the extension imports

**4. npm proxy (Pattern 4)**
- Temporarily remove `@sourcegraph/scip-python` from virtual modules
- Load scip extension with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: extension loads, proxy stub provides no-op responses
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=NpmProxy is emitted

**5. Export shape normalization (Pattern 5)**
- Construct a test extension with CJS-style double-wrapped export
- Load with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: init() is found and called correctly
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=ExportShape is emitted

**6. All repairs disabled**
- Set all RepairConfig flags to false
- Load an extension that needs repair
- Assert: it FAILS (repairs don't fire when disabled)

**7. Repair event audit**
- Load multiple extensions that need different repairs
- Assert: repair_events() returns the correct count and patterns
- Assert: no false-positive repairs (extensions that load cleanly have no events)

**8. New extension validation**
- Download 2-3 extensions NOT in current corpus (from the 24 identified repos)
- Load them with auto-repair enabled
- Document which repairs fire (regression test for future)

### Test Infrastructure
- Uses the same `load_and_validate_extension()` from conformance_report.rs
- Adds `repair_events()` method to the test harness
- Feature-gated: only runs with `--features ext-conformance`
- Each test restores the original state (doesn't permanently remove stubs)

### Success Criteria
- All 8 test scenarios pass
- Repair events are accurate (no false positives)
- At least 1 NEW extension (not in corpus) loads via auto-repair","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:37:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.8.8","title":"Graduate manual stubs: remove hand-written fixes replaced by auto-repair","description":"## What

After auto-repair is implemented and integration-tested, remove the manual
stub files we created to get to 223/223.  The auto-repair pipeline should
handle these cases dynamically, so the stubs become dead code.

## Manual stubs to remove

1. \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/shared/index.ts\`
   - Created for: qualisero-background-notify, qualisero-safe-git
   - Replaced by: Pattern 3 (monorepo escape stub generation)

2. \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/qualisero-pi-agent-scip/dist/extension.js\`
   - Created for: community/qualisero-pi-agent-scip
   - Replaced by: Pattern 1 (dist→src fallback)

3. \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/nicobailon-interview-tool/form/\`
   - Created for: nicobailon-interview-tool
   - Replaced by: Pattern 2 (missing asset fallback)

4. \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/npm/aliou-pi-processes/constants.ts\`
   \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/npm/aliou-pi-processes/utils.ts\`
   \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/npm/aliou-pi-processes/components/processes-component.ts\`
   - Created for: aliou-pi-processes
   - Replaced by: Pattern 3 (monorepo escape) for most, Pattern 2 for some

## Process

1. For each stub, verify that the corresponding auto-repair pattern handles it
2. Remove the stub file
3. Run conformance_full_report to verify the extension still passes
4. Verify the repair event shows the correct pattern
5. Commit the removal

## Why NOT to remove all stubs at once

Some stubs may turn out to be better than auto-repair for specific cases.
For example, the shared/index.ts has carefully typed interfaces that the
auto-generated stub won't have.  If an extension's runtime behavior depends
on the stub having correct types/values, keep the manual stub and document why.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each removable stub is identified and tagged
- [ ] Each removal is verified with a conformance run
- [ ] Stubs that are better kept are documented with reasons
- [ ] No regression in conformance results

## Estimated Effort: 1-2 hours

## Dependencies
- Requires bd-k5q5.8.7 (integration tests passing)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:50.890583943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:48:12.345512927Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auto-repair","cleanup","extensions"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:50.890583943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:48:12.345445561Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:52.831794974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":870,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Details: Graduate Manual Stubs

### What This Means
Once auto-repair is implemented and integration-tested, we can remove the
hand-written stubs that were created to achieve 100% conformance. The auto-repair
system should generate equivalent behavior dynamically.

### Manual Artifacts to Remove

**1. Filesystem Stubs (Pattern 1 & 2 replacements)**
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/qualisero-pi-agent-scip/dist/extension.js`
  → Auto-repair Pattern 1 handles this (dist→src fallback)
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/nicobailon-interview-tool/form/` (7 files)
  → Auto-repair Pattern 2 handles this (missing asset fallback)

**2. Monorepo Stubs (Pattern 3 replacement)**
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/shared/index.ts` (17 exports)
  → Auto-repair Pattern 3 generates this dynamically from import analysis

**3. Virtual Module Stubs (Pattern 4 replacement - SELECTIVE)**
Some virtual modules should be KEPT because they provide nuanced behavior:
- KEEP: node:fs, node:path, node:os (system module shims with real behavior)
- KEEP: `@marckrenn/pi-sub-shared` (has specific MODEL_MULTIPLIERS data)
- REMOVE: Generic stubs that just export empty objects/functions
- Candidates for removal:
  - `@sourcegraph/scip-python` (proxy would work fine)
  - Simple no-op stubs that match the proxy pattern exactly

### Migration Process
For each stub removal:
1. Remove the stub (file or virtual module entry)
2. Run conformance tests with auto-repair enabled
3. Verify the extension still passes
4. Verify the RepairEvent matches the expected pattern
5. If test fails: investigate why and either improve auto-repair OR keep the stub

### Rollback Plan
- Keep all removed stubs in a git tag for easy rollback
- If auto-repair regresses, re-add the affected stubs
- Feature flags allow disabling individual patterns without code changes

### NOT in Scope
- Removing VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json fixes (those correct genuine expectation mismatches)
- Removing crypto_shim.rs (that provides real node:crypto functionality)
- Removing node:* module shims (those provide real behavior, not just stubs)

### Success Criteria
- At least 5 manual stubs removed and replaced by auto-repair
- Conformance still at 223/223 (or higher if new extensions added)
- No manual intervention needed for the graduated extensions
- Repair events logged for each auto-repaired extension","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:37:28Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-k5q5.8.9","title":"Comprehensive unit test suite for all auto-repair patterns","description":"## What

Create a dedicated, organized unit test file (`tests/extensions_auto_repair_unit.rs`) that
provides comprehensive coverage for every auto-repair function, edge case, and error path.

This is DISTINCT from the integration tests (bd-k5q5.8.7) which test whole-extension loading.
Unit tests here validate individual repair FUNCTIONS in isolation with synthetic inputs,
ensuring each function handles all edge cases correctly.

## Why a Dedicated Bead

Each pattern bead (.8.2-.8.6) includes basic tests in its acceptance criteria, but those
are "happy path" tests embedded in the pattern implementation. This bead is about:
1. Systematic edge case coverage that individual pattern authors might miss
2. Organized test structure with shared fixtures and helpers
3. Repair event logging verification (every test checks events are emitted correctly)
4. Feature flag interaction testing (disable one pattern, verify others still work)
5. Cross-pattern interaction testing (multiple repairs on same extension)

## Test Organization

```rust
// tests/extensions_auto_repair_unit.rs

mod test_infrastructure {
    // Shared helpers: create_test_runtime_with_repair_config()
    // Shared fixtures: minimal extension sources for each pattern
    // Assertion helpers: assert_repair_event!(events, pattern, specifier)
    // Logging verification: assert_repair_log_contains!(pattern, message)
}

mod pattern_1_dist_to_src {
    // 8+ tests
    fn dist_js_to_src_ts()           // ./dist/foo.js -> ./src/foo.ts
    fn dist_js_to_src_tsx()          // ./dist/foo.js -> ./src/foo.tsx
    fn dist_js_to_src_js()           // ./dist/foo.js -> ./src/foo.js (JS source)
    fn dist_index_to_src_index()     // ./dist/index.js -> ./src/index.ts
    fn nested_dist_path()            // ./lib/dist/util.js -> ./lib/src/util.ts
    fn no_fallback_when_dist_exists()   // dist/foo.js exists -> use it
    fn no_fallback_when_src_missing()   // neither -> error
    fn no_fallback_when_disabled()      // config flag off
    fn repair_event_emitted()           // verify RepairEvent fields
    fn non_dist_path_unaffected()       // ./lib/foo.js -> no repair
}

mod pattern_2_missing_asset {
    // 10+ tests
    fn missing_html_returns_stub()       // .html -> minimal HTML
    fn missing_css_returns_empty()       // .css -> empty
    fn missing_js_returns_empty()        // .js -> empty
    fn missing_json_still_errors()       // .json -> NOT repaired
    fn missing_env_still_errors()        // .env -> NOT repaired
    fn existing_file_returned_as_is()    // exists -> real content
    fn outside_extension_root_errors()   // /etc/passwd -> error
    fn path_traversal_blocked()          // ../../etc/passwd -> error
    fn symlink_traversal_blocked()       // symlink escape -> error
    fn no_fallback_when_disabled()       // config flag off
    fn multiple_assets_multiple_events() // 7 files -> 7 events
}

mod pattern_3_monorepo_escape {
    // 12+ tests
    fn detect_double_dot_escape()        // ../../shared -> detected
    fn no_detect_within_root()           // ./utils -> NOT escape
    fn analyze_esm_named_imports()       // import { X, Y } from "..."
    fn analyze_esm_default_import()      // import X from "..."
    fn analyze_esm_namespace_import()    // import * as X from "..."
    fn analyze_cjs_destructured()        // const { X } = require("...")
    fn analyze_type_only_skipped()       // import type { X } -> excluded
    fn generate_function_stub()          // getName -> export function
    fn generate_constant_stub()          // UPPER_CASE -> export const
    fn generate_class_stub()             // MyClass -> export class
    fn generate_boolean_heuristic()      // isEnabled -> () => false
    fn no_escape_when_disabled()         // config flag off
}

mod pattern_4_npm_proxy {
    // 14+ tests
    fn proxy_absorbs_property_access()     // proxy.foo -> proxy
    fn proxy_absorbs_nested_access()       // proxy.foo.bar.baz -> proxy
    fn proxy_absorbs_function_call()       // proxy.foo() -> proxy
    fn proxy_absorbs_constructor()         // new proxy.Foo() -> proxy
    fn proxy_toPrimitive_string()          // String(proxy) -> ""
    fn proxy_typeof_function()             // typeof proxy -> "function"
    fn proxy_json_stringify_no_throw()     // JSON.stringify -> safe
    fn proxy_spread_no_throw()             // {...proxy} -> safe
    fn proxy_array_isArray_false()         // Array.isArray(proxy) -> false
    fn allowlist_permits_scoped()          // @aliou/foo -> allowed
    fn allowlist_blocks_node_builtin()     // fs, child_process -> blocked
    fn allowlist_blocks_node_prefixed()    // node:fs -> blocked
    fn existing_virtual_module_priority()  // hand-written wins
    fn no_proxy_when_disabled()            // config flag off
}

mod pattern_5_export_shape {
    // 10+ tests
    fn standard_default_unchanged()      // export default fn -> no repair
    fn unwrap_single_default()           // { default: fn } -> fn
    fn unwrap_double_default()           // { default: { default: fn } } -> fn
    fn activate_named_export()           // activate -> used
    fn init_named_export()               // init -> used
    fn setup_named_export()              // setup -> used
    fn no_callable_clear_error()         // no shape -> descriptive error
    fn object_with_init_method()         // { init: fn } -> as-is
    fn no_norm_when_disabled()           // config flag off
}

mod cross_pattern {
    // 6+ tests
    fn multiple_patterns_single_ext()    // P1 + P3 together
    fn selective_disable_one()           // disable P2, others work
    fn all_disabled_clean_failure()      // all off -> original errors
    fn repair_events_accumulate()        // 3 repairs -> 3 events
    fn repair_count_matches_events()     // count == len
}

mod logging_verification {
    // 4+ tests
    fn event_has_correct_extension_id()
    fn event_has_descriptive_action()    // includes file paths, not just "repaired"
    fn events_serializable_to_json()     // serde_json works
    fn event_timestamp_monotonic()       // timestamps increase
}
```

## Diagnostic Logging in Tests

Every test MUST include diagnostic output:
```rust
fn dist_js_to_src_ts() {
    let config = RepairConfig { dist_to_src: true, ..Default::default() };
    let result = try_dist_to_src_fallback("./dist/extension.js", "/ext/root");
    eprintln!("[test:P1] input=./dist/extension.js result={:?}", result);
    assert_eq!(result, Some(PathBuf::from("/ext/root/src/extension.ts")));
    let events = runtime.drain_repair_events();
    eprintln!("[test:P1] repair_events={:?}", events);
    assert_eq!(events.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(events[0].pattern, RepairPattern::DistToSrc);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 60+ unit tests across all 5 patterns + cross-pattern + logging
- [ ] Every test verifies repair events (emitted or NOT emitted)
- [ ] Every test includes diagnostic eprintln! output
- [ ] Shared test infrastructure (helpers, fixtures, assertion macros)
- [ ] Security edge cases: path traversal, symlinks, system paths
- [ ] All pass with `cargo test --test extensions_auto_repair_unit`

### Shared Helpers Module

```rust
/// Create a PiJsRuntime with auto-repair enabled and specified config
fn create_repair_runtime(config: RepairConfig) -> PiJsRuntime {
    let mut rt_config = PiJsRuntimeConfig::default();
    rt_config.auto_repair_enabled = true;
    rt_config.repair_config = config;
    PiJsRuntime::new(rt_config)
}

/// Create a temp directory with a mock extension structure
fn create_mock_extension(name: &str, files: &[(&str, &str)]) -> TempDir {
    let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
    for (path, content) in files {
        let full = dir.path().join(path);
        std::fs::create_dir_all(full.parent().unwrap()).unwrap();
        std::fs::write(&full, content).unwrap();
    }
    dir
}

/// Assert a repair event was emitted with the expected pattern
macro_rules\! assert_repair_event {
    ($events:expr, $pattern:expr, $contains:expr) => {
        let matching = $events.iter()
            .filter(|e| e.pattern == $pattern)
            .collect::<Vec<_>>();
        assert\!(\!matching.is_empty(),
            "Expected repair event {:?} but got: {:?}", $pattern, $events);
        assert\!(matching.iter().any(|e| e.repair_action.contains($contains)),
            "Expected repair action containing '{}' but got: {:?}",
            $contains, matching);
    };
}

/// Assert NO repair events were emitted
macro_rules\! assert_no_repairs {
    ($events:expr) => {
        assert\!($events.is_empty(),
            "Expected no repair events but got {} events: {:?}",
            $events.len(), $events);
    };
}
```

### Test Fixture Files

Create minimal extension sources as string constants:
```rust
const FIXTURE_DIST_IMPORT: &str = r#"export { default } from './dist/extension.js';"#;
const FIXTURE_SRC_EXTENSION: &str = r#"export default function(pi) { pi.registerCommand({ name: 'test' }); }"#;
const FIXTURE_MONOREPO_ESCAPE: &str = r#"import { getFoo, BAR_CONST } from '../../shared';
export default function(pi) { getFoo(); }"#;
const FIXTURE_CJS_DOUBLE_WRAP: &str = r#"module.exports = { default: function(pi) { pi.registerCommand({ name: 'cjs' }); } };"#;
// ... more fixtures
```

### Diagnostic Output Pattern

Every test follows this logging pattern:
```rust
#[test]
fn pattern_X_scenario_Y() {
    eprintln\!("\n=== TEST: pattern_X_scenario_Y ===");
    eprintln\!("[setup] config={:?}", config);
    eprintln\!("[setup] extension_root={}", dir.path().display());

    let result = /* ... */;
    eprintln\!("[result] {:?}", result);

    let events = runtime.drain_repair_events();
    eprintln\!("[events] count={} events={:?}", events.len(), events);

    // assertions...
    eprintln\!("[PASS] pattern_X_scenario_Y");
}
```

This ensures that when tests fail in CI, the log output is sufficient to diagnose
the failure without reproducing locally.","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:50:37Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-k5q5.8.9","title":"Comprehensive unit test suite for all auto-repair patterns","description":"## What

Create a dedicated, organized unit test file (`tests/extensions_auto_repair_unit.rs`) that
provides comprehensive coverage for every auto-repair function, edge case, and error path.

This is DISTINCT from the integration tests (bd-k5q5.8.7) which test whole-extension loading.
Unit tests here validate individual repair FUNCTIONS in isolation with synthetic inputs,
ensuring each function handles all edge cases correctly.

## Why a Dedicated Bead

Each pattern bead (.8.2-.8.6) includes basic tests in its acceptance criteria, but those
are "happy path" tests embedded in the pattern implementation. This bead is about:
1. Systematic edge case coverage that individual pattern authors might miss
2. Organized test structure with shared fixtures and helpers
3. Repair event logging verification (every test checks events are emitted correctly)
4. Feature flag interaction testing (disable one pattern, verify others still work)
5. Cross-pattern interaction testing (multiple repairs on same extension)

## Test Organization

```rust
// tests/extensions_auto_repair_unit.rs

mod test_infrastructure {
    // Shared helpers: create_test_runtime_with_repair_config()
    // Shared fixtures: minimal extension sources for each pattern
    // Assertion helpers: assert_repair_event!(events, pattern, specifier)
    // Logging verification: assert_repair_log_contains!(pattern, message)
}

mod pattern_1_dist_to_src {
    // 8+ tests
    fn dist_js_to_src_ts()           // ./dist/foo.js -> ./src/foo.ts
    fn dist_js_to_src_tsx()          // ./dist/foo.js -> ./src/foo.tsx
    fn dist_js_to_src_js()           // ./dist/foo.js -> ./src/foo.js (JS source)
    fn dist_index_to_src_index()     // ./dist/index.js -> ./src/index.ts
    fn nested_dist_path()            // ./lib/dist/util.js -> ./lib/src/util.ts
    fn no_fallback_when_dist_exists()   // dist/foo.js exists -> use it
    fn no_fallback_when_src_missing()   // neither -> error
    fn no_fallback_when_disabled()      // config flag off
    fn repair_event_emitted()           // verify RepairEvent fields
    fn non_dist_path_unaffected()       // ./lib/foo.js -> no repair
}

mod pattern_2_missing_asset {
    // 10+ tests
    fn missing_html_returns_stub()       // .html -> minimal HTML
    fn missing_css_returns_empty()       // .css -> empty
    fn missing_js_returns_empty()        // .js -> empty
    fn missing_json_still_errors()       // .json -> NOT repaired
    fn missing_env_still_errors()        // .env -> NOT repaired
    fn existing_file_returned_as_is()    // exists -> real content
    fn outside_extension_root_errors()   // /etc/passwd -> error
    fn path_traversal_blocked()          // ../../etc/passwd -> error
    fn symlink_traversal_blocked()       // symlink escape -> error
    fn no_fallback_when_disabled()       // config flag off

}

    fn analyze_esm_namespace_import()    // import * as X from "..."
    fn analyze_cjs_destructured()        // const { X } = require("...")
    fn analyze_type_only_skipped()       // import type { X } -> excluded
    fn generate_function_stub()          // getName -> export function
    fn generate_constant_stub()          // UPPER_CASE -> export const
    fn generate_class_stub()             // MyClass -> export class
    fn generate_boolean_heuristic()      // isEnabled -> () => false
    fn no_escape_when_disabled()         // config flag off
}

mod pattern_4_npm_proxy {
    // 14+ tests

    fn proxy_absorbs_constructor()         // new proxy.Foo() -> proxy
    fn proxy_toPrimitive_string()          // String(proxy) -> ""
    fn proxy_typeof_function()             // typeof proxy -> "function"
    fn proxy_json_stringify_no_throw()     // JSON.stringify -> safe
    fn proxy_spread_no_throw()             // {...proxy} -> safe
    fn proxy_array_isArray_false()         // Array.isArray(proxy) -> false
    fn allowlist_permits_scoped()          // @aliou/foo -> allowed
    fn allowlist_blocks_node_builtin()     // fs, child_process -> blocked
    fn allowlist_blocks_node_prefixed()    // node:fs -> blocked
    fn existing_virtual_module_priority()  // hand-written wins
    fn no_proxy_when_disabled()            // config flag off
}

mod pattern_5_export_shape {
    // 10+ tests
    fn standard_default_unchanged()      // export default fn -> no repair
    fn unwrap_single_default()           // { default: fn } -> fn
    fn unwrap_double_default()           // { default: { default: fn } } -> fn
    fn activate_named_export()           // activate -> used
    fn init_named_export()               // init -> used
    fn setup_named_export()              // setup -> used
    fn no_callable_clear_error()         // no shape -> descriptive error
    fn object_with_init_method()         // { init: fn } -> as-is
    fn no_norm_when_disabled()           // config flag off
}

mod cross_pattern {

    fn all_disabled_clean_failure()      // all off -> original errors
```

 {"id":"bd-k5q5.9","title":"[LISR] Dynamic Secure Extension Repair Program (Intent-Legible Self-Healing)","description":"This program introduces a secure, deterministic, and auditable mechanism to dynamically repair broken extensions when intent is legible and risk can be bounded. Current extension breakage often comes from schema drift, API renames, and minor structural issues; we want a first-class self-healing path that reduces operator toil without compromising safety.

This epic exists to coordinate all required workstreams: policy and threat boundaries, legibility scoring, deterministic rewrites, constrained model assistance, behavior proofs, controlled rollout, and operational observability.","design":"Architecture is phase-gated and fail-closed:
1) Security contract and policy modes define what is allowed.
2) Intent legibility pipeline decides whether auto-repair is even eligible.
3) Deterministic rule engine performs known-safe rewrites first.
4) Optional model-assisted proposal stage is constrained by a primitive whitelist.
5) Proof pipeline verifies behavioral and capability invariants.
6) Overlay canary rollout validates production safety before promotion.
7) Full audit and telemetry enable forensic confidence and operator trust.","acceptance_criteria":"- End-to-end repair path exists from broken extension input to safe promoted output.
- Repair never increases privilege without explicit human approval.
- Validation/proof pipeline blocks unsafe or ambiguous repairs.
- Canary/rollback controls are automated and deterministic.
- Audit trail is complete enough for security review and incident forensics.","notes":"Strategic justification:
- Improves reliability and user trust in extension ecosystem.
- Reduces mean-time-to-recovery for breakages caused by upstream/API drift.
- Preserves project principle: safety and determinism over convenience.

Non-goals:
- No unconstrained AI patching.
- No silent privilege expansion.
- No executing untrusted broken code to infer intent.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:13.622771644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:58:35.295017028Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:58:35.294925958Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","lisr","program","repair","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:13.622771644Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":833,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program context: this issue captures the complete self-healing strategy for extension repair when intent is legible and risk is bounded. Every child issue includes embedded rationale so future contributors do not need external planning documents.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:08Z"},{"id":845,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution map and dependency rationale:\n\nPhase A (Policy foundation): LISR-1 defines mode semantics, static-only boundaries, and privilege monotonicity. Every downstream epic depends on this because no repair behavior is acceptable without enforceable safety policy.\n\nPhase B (Eligibility and deterministic repair): LISR-2 determines whether intent is legible enough to repair. LISR-3 performs deterministic rewrites for known breakage classes. These two epics establish the safe default path and reduce reliance on model assistance.\n\nPhase C (Constrained AI path): LISR-4 is explicitly downstream of LISR-2 and LISR-3. We only allow model proposals after deterministic options and confidence gating are available, and only through strict primitive schemas.\n\nPhase D (Proof before activation): LISR-5 blocks production rollout. Structural checks, capability monotonicity, hostcall parity, and conformance replay/golden checksums are the hard activation gate.\n\nPhase E (Runtime rollout safety): LISR-6 adds overlay artifacts, canary routing, and deterministic rollback. This assumes proof pipeline outputs are trustworthy and machine-readable.\n\nPhase F (Operational trust): LISR-7 captures append-only ledger, telemetry, operator inspection, and forensic bundles to support incident response and governance.\n\nPhase G (Institutional memory): LISR-8 codifies ADRs, playbooks, developer guidance, and release governance so system intent survives team turnover.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:34:43Z"},{"id":856,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Detailed phase rationale (human-readable version):

Phase A - Policy Foundation:
LISR-1 is upstream of all work because no repair automation is acceptable without mode semantics, static-only boundaries, and privilege monotonicity controls.

Phase B - Legibility + Deterministic Repair:
LISR-2 determines eligibility (is intent legible enough?) and LISR-3 provides deterministic, auditable repair behavior for common breakage classes.

Phase C - Constrained AI Assist:
LISR-4 is intentionally downstream of LISR-2 and LISR-3 so model usage remains bounded, policy-governed, and secondary to deterministic fixes.

Phase D - Proof Gate:
LISR-5 is the hard activation barrier. Candidates must pass structural checks, security invariants, semantic parity checks, and conformance replay.

Phase E - Runtime Safety:
LISR-6 delivers canary and rollback controls to handle real-world runtime regressions even after offline proof passes.

Phase F - Trust and Operations:
LISR-7 ensures every decision is observable, explainable, and reconstructable for incidents.

Phase G - Long-Term Maintainability:
LISR-8 captures rationale, operator procedures, and governance so future teams can evolve the system safely.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:35:25Z"},{"id":896,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Reopened to activate planned execution lane; prior closed state did not reflect current work start intent.","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:42:38Z"},{"id":917,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution lane activated: starting with LISR-1 policy foundation. Root and LISR-1 are set to in_progress, with LISR-1.1 claimed as the current implementation focus.","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:42:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","title":"[LISR-1] Security Contract and Repair Policy Framework","description":"Define non-negotiable safety constraints, policy modes, and privilege boundaries that govern all repair behavior.","design":"Codify mode semantics (off/suggest/auto-safe/auto-strict), static-only constraints, privilege monotonicity, and high-risk approval requirements as machine-enforced rules.","acceptance_criteria":"Policy engine enforces fail-closed behavior, mode semantics are test-covered, and all downstream repair flows consume the same authoritative policy decisions.","notes":"This is the foundational trust layer. If this is weak, the entire self-healing system is unsafe regardless of model quality or rewrite correctness.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:23.580113284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:57:46.731745812Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:57:46.731622602Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","lisr","policy","repair","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:23.580113284Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":834,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Security-first ordering is intentional: no repair implementation should proceed without policy and threat constraints being machine-enforced.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:08Z"},{"id":846,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency intent for LISR-1:\n- 1.1 is the control-plane entry point (mode resolution).\n- 1.2, 1.3, and 1.4 all depend on 1.1 because behavior must be mode-aware and deterministic.\n- This epic is upstream of all other LISR epics by design; it defines what is allowed before discussing what is possible.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:34:43Z"},{"id":857,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LISR-1 sequencing note:
- 1.1 defines authoritative mode resolution.
- 1.2/1.3/1.4 consume that mode context.
This enforces one control plane for safety posture.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:35:27Z"},{"id":895,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Reopened to activate planned execution lane; prior closed state did not reflect current work start intent.","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:42:38Z"},{"id":918,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Lane plan for this epic:\n1) Implement LISR-1.1 mode and config precedence plus diagnostics.\n2) Then advance LISR-1.2 static-only boundary.\n3) Then LISR-1.3 privilege monotonicity checker.\n4) Then LISR-1.4 high-risk approval gates.\nDependencies intentionally enforce this sequence.","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:42:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","title":"[LISR-1.1] Implement repair policy modes and config/CLI resolution","description":"Implement unified configuration for repair modes (off, suggest, auto-safe, auto-strict) with deterministic precedence across defaults, config, env, and CLI.","design":"Extend config schema and CLI plumbing so mode resolution is transparent and testable; include explicit diagnostics for active mode and source.","acceptance_criteria":"Mode resolution is deterministic, documented, and fully covered by precedence tests; invalid values fail fast with actionable errors.","notes":"This prevents hidden behavior drift and ensures operators always know which risk posture is active.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:29.978592666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:57:05.352085138Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:57:05.351940378Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","extensions","lisr","policy","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:29.978592666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":842,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction note: mode names were normalized after shell interpolation noise; canonical policy modes are off/suggest/auto-safe/auto-strict.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:59Z"},{"id":891,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Reopened to activate planned execution lane; prior closed state did not reflect current work start intent.","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:42:38Z"},{"id":919,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed as current work item for lane start. Exit criteria for handoff to 1.2/1.3/1.4: deterministic precedence tests pass, invalid mode handling is explicit, and active-mode diagnostics are emitted from one canonical resolution path.","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:42:40Z"}]}
